item.title: Sysco Nears $29 Billion Deal to Buy Restaurant Depot, WSJ Says
item.description: Sysco Corp., a large US food distributor, is close to a deal to buy privately held Restaurant Depot LLC for about $29 billion including debt, according to the Wall Street Journal.

item.title: Wise to Open Everyday Banking Accounts in UK in Deposit Push
item.description: Wise Plc is rolling out everyday bank accounts in the UK as it seeks to take up more market share from longtime banks and other fintechs.

item.title: Weak Rupee Likely to Have Mixed Impact on Economy, Minister Says
item.description: The impact of the rupee’s depreciation on India’s economy will likely be mixed, with the country’s import bill expected to be higher while export competitiveness may improve, a junior finance minister said.

item.title: Bond Investors Suddenly Are More Focused on Growth Than Inflation
item.description: With war seen weighing on economy, Treasuries start to attract buyers.

item.title: Inside the Odd—and Oddly Human—Work of Teaching AI to Talk
item.description: They vent, confess and role-play with strangers, all to help machines learn how to sound human.

item.title: Chicago Area Grapples With Property-Tax Growth Double Inflation
item.description: Chicago-area residents are facing some of the country’s highest property taxes, with bills surging at double the inflation rate over the past 30 years, according to a study released Monday.

item.title: Euro Poised for Worst Quarter Since 2024 as Oil Shock Bites
item.description: The euro is heading for its worst quarter since 2024 as war in the Middle East underscores Europe’s dependency on energy imports and rattles the region’s economic outlook.

item.title: BBVA Plans SRT Tied to €3 Billion of Mortgages Amid Mideast War
item.description: BBVA SA is working on a significant risk transfer tied to a portfolio of mortgages as banks press ahead with such deals amid the ongoing war in the Middle East.

item.title: Goldman Traders See Signs Hedge Funds Are Capitulating on Stocks
item.description: Heavy short sales by hedge funds and disposals by systematic investors have increased the potential for a sharp swing higher for stocks in the event of a de-escalation in the Iran war, according to traders at Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: European Consumers’ Inflation Expectations Surge on Iran War
item.description: Inflation expectations of euro-area consumers jumped in March — a warning sign for the European Central Bank as it assesses the danger of a renewed price spike due to the Iran war.

item.title: Portugal Sees TAP Interest Hold Despite War as Deadline Nears
item.description: Portugal is confident Europe’s three biggest airline groups will bid for a stake in TAP SA ahead of a deadline this week, brushing aside concerns that the Iran war is fueling oil volatility and uncertainty across the aviation sector.

item.title: Iran’s Grip on Hormuz Is Tighter Than Ever After a Month of War
item.description: Israel and US strikes have wiped out senior Iranian leaders and hit key targets across the country. But after a month of fighting, it is arguably Iran that has secured the most significant strategic victory — a tightening grip over traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Morgan Stanley’s Wilson Says S&P 500 Correction Nears End Stage
item.description: The S&P 500 correction is nearing its final stage even as the Iran war continues, according to strategists at Morgan Stanley, who warn that Federal Reserve interest-rate hikes still pose a threat to stocks.

item.title: Universal Music Announces First-Ever Buyback After Share Slump
item.description: Universal Music Group NV plans to repurchase €500 million ($575 million) of its shares, the first ever buyback since the record label was listed in 2021.

item.title: Victory Giant Is Said to Gauge Investor Interest for HK Listing
item.description: Victory Giant Technology (Huizhou) Co. has started gauging investor interest for a Hong Kong listing, according to people familiar with the matter, getting the ball rolling for what may be among the city’s largest deals this year.

item.title: German Inflation Headed for Highest in More Than Year on Energy
item.description: German inflation probably surged to the highest level in more than a year after the Iran war propelled energy costs.

item.title: Airbus and L3Harris Win $1.1 Billion Deals for Canada Fleet Support
item.description: The Canadian government awarded three long-term support contracts worth about C$1.5 billion ($1.1 billion) to L3Harris Technologies Inc. and Airbus SE as part of its push to increase defense spending.

item.title: SMFG Cools on Acquisitions as Banking Giant Sets New Profit Goal
item.description: Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group Inc. is targeting profits to increase by about a third as it seeks growth without large acquisitions, a shift from its appetite for deals that had driven billion-dollar transactions in recent years.

item.title: France Broadens Energy Aid to Additional 700,000 Households
item.description: French Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu said more households will benefit from energy aid this year after the government identified additional eligible recipients.

item.title: UK Mortgage Approvals Rose Before War Triggered Rates Surge
item.description: Approvals for UK home loans climbed for the first time in five months, a sign housing demand was gaining momentum before the mortgage market was rattled by the war in Iran.

item.title: US, Israel Keep Up Strikes on Iran After Troops Arrive in Region
item.description: US and Israeli forces pressed ahead with attacks on Iran while the Islamic Republic launched missiles across the Persian Gulf, sending oil prices higher once again amid no sign of an imminent peace deal.

item.title: Hong Kong Tech ETF Draws Record Bets as Investors Defy Jitters
item.description: Hong Kong’s second-largest exchange traded fund, focused on Chinese tech stocks, saw record inflows in March, highlighting investors’ pursuit of promising growth opportunities while weighing Iran war jitters.

item.title: European Bank Stocks to Snap Record Quarterly Run on Iran, AI
item.description: European banking shares are poised to snap a record 13-quarter winning streak, as optimism over strong earnings and capital returns is displaced by concerns over the impact of the Iran war and an exodus from the private credit market.

item.title: Goldman CIO Marco Argenti on the Warp-Speed Improvements in AI
item.description: Look how much has changed in just a year and a half.

item.title: Odd Lots: Goldman’s Argenti on the Improvements in AI (Podcast)
item.description: When we last spoke to Marco Argenti, chief information officer at Goldman Sachs, we were talking about how the bank was deploying AI, including the development of its own internal tools. But that was a year and a half ago and a lot has changed since then, especially with the arrival of agentic platforms like Claude Code. So what exactly is Goldman Sachs doing with AI now? And what has its experience with the new tech been like so far? On this episode, we catch up with Marco to discuss what AI de

item.title: Qatar Central Bank Allows Loan Deferrals as Iran War Drags On
item.description: Qatar’s central bank is allowing lenders to offer borrowers payment deferrals, cutting reserve requirements and providing unlimited repo liquidity as Gulf policymakers race to respond to fallouts from the Iran war that’s now in its fifth week.

item.title: Swiss Find They Can’t Pay With Cash as Much as They Used to
item.description: People in Switzerland are increasingly frustrated that they can’t withdraw and use cash as much as they used to.

item.title: Saudi Oil Price Talks Take on Added Urgency as Deadline Nears
item.description: The Iran war has thrown conventional pricing for Saudi Arabian crude into disarray, with tense buyers in Asia seeking to steer the kingdom toward alternative mechanisms for supplies as oil prices soar.

item.title: Sri Lanka in Talks for Russian Oil Products as War Jolts Market
item.description: Sri Lanka’s state-run Ceylon Petroleum Corp. is in talks with Russian oil companies to import petroleum products, as the Middle East war constrains flows, boosts prices and prompts buyers to look for cargoes.

item.title: Kaefer Owners Study Sale of Company at More Than €2 Billion Value
item.description: The owners of Kaefer are exploring a sale of the German industrial services company, which could be valued at up more than €2 billion ($2.3 billion), people familiar with the matter said.
